finals.clue: Originally recorded by Édith Piaf, this song about finding beauty in life was later covered by Grace Jones and has become one of the most recognizable French chansons in the world.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which song, originally recorded by Édith Piaf, was later covered by Grace Jones?' with answer 'La Vie En Rose'.

finals.title: CLASSIC ROCK ALBUMS
finals.clue: This British rock band's fourth studio album had no title or band name on its cover, yet it sold millions of copies and contains 'Stairway to Heaven'.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which rock band's fourth album had no title or name on the cover, but sold millions of copies?' and source answer is 'Led Zeppelin'; the album is commonly known as Led Zeppelin IV.

finals.title: REAL NAMES IN MUSIC
finals.clue: Born Roberta Joan Anderson in 1943, this Canadian singer-songwriter is celebrated for albums like 'Blue' and 'Court and Spark', and is widely regarded as one of the greatest songwriters of the 20th century.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Whose Real Name Is Roberta Anderson?' and source answer is 'Joni Mitchell'; the real-name theme is stated explicitly in the source question text.

finals.title: ROCK & ROLL HALL OF FAME
finals.clue: He was the very first performer inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 1986, and his only UK number-one single was 'My Ding A Ling' in 1972.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Who was the very first performer to be inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 1986?' and source answer is 'Chuck Berry'. Second source (tapi:622a1c357cc59eab6f94fd60) confirms Chuck Berry's only UK No.1 was 'My Ding A Ling', adding a confirming detail to the clue.

finals.title: MICHAEL JACKSON
finals.clue: The actor Vincent Price provided the chilling spoken-word monologue on this Michael Jackson album, which went on to become the best-selling album of all time according to the RIAA.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which actor did the voice-over on the Michael Jackson song Thriller?'; answer is 'Vincent Price'. A second source [tapi:622a1c367cc59eab6f950326] confirms the album 'Thriller' featured Vincent Price and sold over 20 million copies. The final clue combines both verified facts — Vincent Price's appearance and the album's record-breaking sales — with the answer 'Thriller' matching both source answers exactly.
